_comment: Single source of truth for the Docker Scout 'clean bill of health' image set. Every image under `required` must earn a grade-A Docker Scout health score. CI enforces this in two places, both driven by this file (via the scout-config job): (1) PR time, .github/workflows/build.yml gates the policies that are deterministically checkable on a locally-built image -- fixable CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs (via scripts/scout-cve-gate.sh) and the default-non-root-user policy; (2) release time, .github/workflows/publish.yml runs `docker scout policy --exit-code` on the PUSHED image, which is the only place the true grade can be evaluated because supply-chain attestations (15 pts) only attach on push and base-image / high-profile-CVE / license policies are computed registry-side. `cve_net_extra` lists extra builder images that get the fixable CRITICAL/HIGH CVE net only, NOT the full grade-A bill of health. A required image may WAIVE a specific fixable finding it has assessed as not exploitable by adding an OpenVEX `not_affected` statement under .github/vex/ (scoped to the image + CVE); the PR gate (scout-cve-gate.sh) lists findings and drops VEX-waived ones, fail-closed -- an auditable, standards-based assessment, not a silent allowlist. `exempt` lists images deliberately left OUT of the grade-A set with a documented reason -- each fails ONLY on third-party-bundled, upstream-frozen CVEs whose vulnerable code IS in the image's execute path (so a VEX not_affected would be false), and which buildenv cannot patch in place. The non-root audit still reports exempt images; they are simply not hard-gated. Exempt-image remediations and active VEX waivers (with their drop triggers) are tracked in docs/upstream-cve-backlog.md. To require a new service, add it to `required` (and add the matching non-root USER + clean deps first). See README 'Image security policies'.

exempt.build-e2e: Assembly image: COPYs already-published third-party release artifacts (martin/shirotester/pdfserv Go binaries, newman's npm deps, wkhtmltopdf's patched-Qt .deb) carrying ~70 fixable CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs buildenv cannot patch without upstream rebuilds. The vulnerable code runs (these are executed tools/binaries), so VEX not_affected does not apply. Grade A is unreachable regardless of base hygiene; keeps its root default (a non-root flip earned no payoff). Upstream backlog: docs/upstream-cve-backlog.md.
exempt.build-js: azure-cli's vendored Python deps carry 2 fixable HIGH (pyjwt 2.12.0 -> 2.13.0 CVE-2026-48526; cryptography 46.0.7 -> 48.0.1 GHSA-537c-gmf6-5ccf). az actually uses these libs (vulnerable code is in the execute path -> not a VEX not_affected case). No buildenv lever: azure-cli 2.87.0 is already the latest release and still bundles the vulnerable versions; Microsoft must bump them. The big golang/stdlib cluster (was 4C + ~25H, from Debian's EOL-Go git-lfs) IS fixed here (official git-lfs binary). Stays non-root. Re-promote when a newer azure-cli ships patched deps. Upstream backlog: docs/upstream-cve-backlog.md.
exempt.build-swaggercodegen: All residual CVEs (1 CRITICAL + 16 HIGH) live inside the third-party swagger-codegen-cli-v3:3.0.34 JAR (snakeyaml, jackson-core, logback, commons-beanutils, commons-io, handlebars) -- libraries the codegen tool actually runs (execute path -> not a VEX not_affected case). buildenv only COPYs the jar; the fixed versions (e.g. commons-beanutils 1.11.0, handlebars 4.5.2) are too recent for the swagger-codegen 3.0.x line to have adopted, so even the latest 3.0.x jar would not fully clear them. Stays non-root. Re-promote if/when a swagger-codegen jar ships clean deps. Upstream backlog: docs/upstream-cve-backlog.md.
